# Manage alias suffixes

When you create an alias in an org or project scope, you must also create a suffix for the scope.
Suffixes provide scope administrators with granular control over the resources in their scopes.
They also allow you to reuse alias values across org and project scopes because the suffix makes them globally unique values.

You can edit or delete existing suffixes.

## Permissions

@include '/suffixes/permissions.mdx'

## Edit a suffix

You can edit or update suffixes.
Existing aliases continue to use the old suffix, any new aliases use the new suffix.
If an existing alias is updated, it will use the new suffix going forward.

Complete the following steps to edit a suffix:

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="UI" group="ui">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Select **Orgs** in the navigation pane, and then select the org or project scope that contains the suffix you want to edit.
1. Click on the suffix in the right sidebar, and then click **Edit suffix**.
1. In the **Alias suffix** field, type a unique value for the suffix. Boundary will append this suffix to any aliases you create within the org or project scope.
1. Click **Save**.
1. Confirm the change.

</Tab>

<Tab heading="CLI" group="cli">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Use the following command to edit an existing alias suffix:

   ```shell-session
   $ boundary scopes set-alias-suffix \
       -id o_1234567890 \
       -alias-suffix org1
   ```

   You must include the following attributes when you edit a suffix:

   - `-id=<string>` - Scope in which to update the suffix.
   You can also specify the scope using the BOUNDARY_SCOPE_ID environment variable.
   - `-alias-suffix=<string>` - Specifies the string that you want to use as the suffix to append to aliases in the designated scope.
   The `alias-suffix` can be a hostname or a DNS-like string.

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## Delete a suffix

You can delete a suffix if you no longer need it.
Before deleting it, make sure no associated aliases are using the suffix.

Boundary prevents you from deleting a suffix if any aliases are using it.
Org suffixes can be associated with aliases in projects nested under the org.
You must delete any associated aliases before deleting the suffix.

Complete the following steps to delete a suffix:

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="UI" group="ui">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Select **Orgs** in the navigation pane, and then select the org or project scope that contains the suffix you want to edit.
1. Click on the suffix in the right sidebar, and then click **Delete suffix**.
1. Confirm that you want to delete the suffix.

</Tab>

<Tab heading="CLI" group="cli">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Use the following command to delete an alias suffix:

   ```shell-session
   $ boundary scopes remove-alias-suffix \
       -id o_1234567890 \
   ```

   You must provide the following attribute when you delete a suffix:

   - `-id=<string>` - Scope from which to delete the suffix.
   You can also specify the scope using the BOUNDARY_SCOPE_ID environment variable.

</Tab>
</Tabs>
